original: Ono no Imoko is dispatched as envoy to the Sui court in China (Traditional Japanese date: July 3, 607)
Peace of Koblenz: Charles the Bald, Louis the German, and Lotharius II
Aghlabid emir Ibrahim II of Ifriqiya destroys Taormina, Sicily
Synod of Pavia: Emperor Henry II convicts married priest
The People's Crusade led by Peter the Hermit arrives at Constantinople with 30,000 followers
Alexius IV Angelus is appointed co-regent of Byzantium
The Everlasting League is signed by Uri, Schwyz, and Unterwalden, forming the basis of the Swiss Confederation (independence)
Dutch Count Ernest Casimir I, Duke of Nassau-Dietz, conquers Oldenzaal
French Ursuline nun Marie de l’Incarnation arrives in Quebec on board the Saint-Joseph, goes on to open the first school for girls, and plays a large part in establishing Catholicism in New France
Battle of St. Gotthard, West Hungary: Imperial Army led by Count Raimondo Montecuccoli defeats Ottoman forces under Köprülü Fazıl Ahmed Pasha
Huron chief Kondiaronk urges First Nations people to sign a peace settlement with France and dies the next day, resulting in the Great Peace of Montreal
First Doggett's Coat and Badge race (Watermen's race) is held on the River Thames (London Bridge to Chelsea)
Nicholas Rowe is appointed British Poet Laureate byGeorge I

In a period known as the South Sea Bubble, the South Sea Company reaches a frenzy in London as the stock price of the South Sea Company peaks at £1,000, collapses soon after, and falls to £124 by December
Foundations are laid for the Bank of England
Battle of Minden, Westphalia: Anglo-German army under Prussian Field Marshal Ferdinand of Brunswick defeats a French army led by Marshal of France Marquis de Contades
Battle of Kagul: smaller Russian army defeats larger Ottoman and Cossack force in Moldavia, the major land battle of the Russo-Turkish War and one of the largest battles of the 18th century
Sweden joins the First League of Armed Neutrality
Caroline Herschel becomes the first woman to discover a comet
US Customs begins enforcing the Tariff Act
France becomes the first country to use the metric system
Whiskey Rebellion begins in western Pennsylvania
Rare tornado hits Westchester County, New York
Dutch King William I accepts blame for Southern defeat
London's Regent's Canal opens
England's Bolton and Leigh Railway opens to freight traffic
London Bridge across the Thames opens to traffic
The Anatomy Act in the UK ends the practice of body snatching from graves by allowing the use of unclaimed bodies from prisons, workhouses, and hospitals
The apprenticeship system is abolished in most of the British Empire, and former slaves are no longer indentured to former owners
Lombard Street Riot erupts in Philadelphia
Rotherhithe Tunnel under the Thames opens
San Francisco Methodists establish Zion Methodist church, the first Black church
Castle Garden in New York City, now known as Castle Clinton, opens as the first US receiving station for immigrants
Brazil recognizes the Confederate States of America
Cavalry action near Brandy Station marks the end of the Gettysburg Campaign
Black people vote for the first time in a US state election in the South (Tennessee)
Irish Land Act gives rights to tenants of landlords in Ireland
Colorado becomes the 38th state of the Union
US Quarantine Station authorized for Angel Island, San Francisco Bay
A papyrus is offered to the British Museum for £10,000, later revealed to be a forgery
Amsterdam's Grand Hotel Krasnapolsky gets electric lights
Inland postal service begins in Great Britain
Great Britain annexes the Kermadec Islands near New Zealand
The roof of Seville's cathedral collapses after an earthquake
Cecil Rhodes' colonists reach Lundi
Burials within San Francisco city limits are prohibited
Building begins on Dutch public housing
Mine accident in Wollongong, Australia, kills 100 people
Brooklyn Superbas MLB pitcher Harry McIntire no-hits Pittsburgh for 10 2/3 innings but loses in the 13th inning 1-0 on an unearned run
First Scout camp opens on Brownsea Island in Poole Harbour, Dorset
U.S. Army Signal Corps establishes a small aeronautical division
Transportation workers begin a major strike in England as part of a wave of industrial unrest
British Grand Fleet reaches Scapa Flow
France and Germany mobilize their troops at outbreak of WWI
Trois Vierges: German 69th infantry regiment enters Luxembourg on outbreak of WWI
Hawaii National Park forms
Frank Little, an American Industrial Workers of the World labor union organizer, is tortured and lynched by unknown assailants in Butte, Montana
British troops enter Vladivostok
Pittsburgh and Boston Braves play an MLB record 20 scoreless innings before the Pirates win 2-0 in the 21st inning
Dutch editor and politician Hendrikus Colijn becomes political editor-in-chief of The Standard
In Italy, the socialist Alleanza del Lavoro declares a national strike, which collapses immediately as fascist forces destroy union and socialist headquarters
MLB Brooklyn Robins pitcher Dazzy Vance strikes out an MLB record 7 consecutive batters and 14 overall in a 4-0 win over the visiting Chicago Cubs at Ebbets Field
Shortwave radio link between Kootwijk and Netherlands East Indies
Battles between Druze and French in Damascus
Failed assassination on General Primo de Rivera in Barcelona
Earliest date for a film to be considered for an Academy Award
Americans sweep medals at a rainy Olympic pole vault final in Amsterdam, with Sabin Carr winning gold, followed by William Droegemuller and Charles McGinnis
George Washington quarter goes into circulation
Death penalty for anti-fascists in Germany
US Amateur Golf Championship format changes to a 6-day event with no 36-hole stroke-play qualifying and 36-hole matches for semi-finals and final
Benjamin Mays named president of Morehouse College
Luftwaffe bombs German 23rd Infantry Division
The first Jeep is produced
American Federation of Musicians labor union begins a strike over royalty payment disagreements, refusing to perform on commercial recordings; the ban lasts over 2 years
Nazi German occupiers demand listing of all Dutch telephone subscribers
Race riots in Harlem, New York City
British Sunderland seaplanes sink German submarines U-454 and U-383
Japan declares Burma independence under U Ba Maw
Race riot in Harlem, New York City
General Montgomery takes command of the 12th and 21st Armies
Polish resistance fighters of the Home Army launch the Warsaw Uprising, the largest military effort undertaken by a resistance movement in occupied Europe
US 90th Division occupies Saint-Hilaire-du-Harcouët in Normandy
US troops enter Tessy-sur-Vire in Normandy
Japanese city Toyama is destroyed by B-29 bombers
US Air Force Office of Special Investigations is founded
American Bowling Congress ends all-white-male rule
Guam Organic Act establishes Guam as an unincorporated US territory
King Leopold of Belgium abdicates, and Baudouin becomes king
Boston Red Sox pitcher Ben Flowers sets then MLB record of eight consecutive games in relief
California introduces a sales tax for education
KMBC TV channel 9 in Kansas City, Missouri (MET/ABC) begins broadcasting
KOBI TV channel 5 in Medford, Oregon (NBC) begins broadcasting
Northern Rhodesia becomes part of Federation of Rhodesia and Nyasaland
South African Natives Resettlement Act comes into effect, empowering the government to remove Africans from any area within and adjacent to the magisterial district of Johannesburg
WKBT TV channel 8 in La Crosse, WI (CBS) begins broadcasting
First microgravity research begins
WILL TV channel 12 in Urbana-Champaign, Illinois (PBS) begins broadcasting
KRCR TV channel 7 in Redding-Chico, California (ABC) begins broadcasting
Ex-MLB outfielder Glen Gorbous throws a regulation baseball a record 136 m (445 ft and 10 in) in a game promotion
First commercial building heated by the Sun (Albuquerque, New Mexico)
US and Canada create North American Air Defense Command (NORAD)
Nuclear-powered submarine USS Nautilus begins the first transit of the North Pole in Operation Sunshine
US first-class postage increases to 4 cents (it had been 3 cents for 26 years)
WAAY TV channel 31 in Huntsville, AL (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
Benin (formerly Dahomey) gains independence from France
Islamabad is declared the capital of Pakistan
German DR limits traffic to West Berlin
New San Francisco Hall of Justice opens
Whitney Young Jr. is named executive director of the National Urban League
Boston Red Sox pitcher Bill Monbouquette no-hits the Chicago White Sox in a 1-0 win at White Sox Park
The Beatles Book sells out on its first day of sale
West Germany annexes the village of Elten
WQAD TV channel 8 in Moline, Illinois (ABC) begins broadcasting
ALM (Antillian Airlines) begins operation
Beatles' single "A Hard Day's Night" goes to #1 and stays at #1 for 2 weeks
Dan "Big Daddy" Garlits becomes the first drag racer to run the 1/4 mile at over 200 mph (201.34 mph [324 km/h] at Island Dragway, New Jersey)
Charles Whitman wounds 31 and kills 16 at University of Texas
Izaak Samkalden becomes mayor of Amsterdam
WBRA TV channel 15 in Roanoke, VA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Canada begins replacing silver with nickel in its coins
Cleveland Metroparks begins operating the zoo in Cleveland, Ohio
Coronation of Hassanal Bolkiah, the 29th Sultan of Brunei
WXIX TV channel 19 in Cincinnati-Newport, OH (IND) begins broadcasting
36th Chicago Charities College All-Star Game: NY Jets 26, All-Stars 24 (74,208 attendees)
Mariner 6 sends close-up photos of Mars
EAA Convention moves from Rockford, IL, to Oshkosh, WI
KTSD TV channel 10 in Pierre, SD (PBS) begins broadcasting
CBS presents the first episode of BBC production "The Six Wives of Henry VIII" and continues weekly until September 5th
Nate Colbert ties the MLB record by hitting five home runs and sets a record with 13 RBIs as the Padres beat Atlanta 9-0 and 11-7 in a doubleheader
Johan Witteveen appointed as the fifth managing director of the International Monetary Fund
MLB All Star catchers Thurman Munson of the NY Yankees and Boston Red Sox's Carlton Fisk brawl at home plate at Fenway Park; Red Sox win 3-2
China's People's Liberation Army Navy puts into service Changzheng 1, its first nuclear-powered submarine
41st Chicago Charities College All-Star Game: Pittsburgh 21, All-Stars 14 (54,103 attendees)
CSCE Final Act creates the Conference for Security and Co-operation in Europe
Thirty-five countries (33 European nations, Canada, and the U.S.) sign the Helsinki Final Act agreement concerning boundary integrity, human rights, and economic cooperation
Flash flood in Big Thompson Canyon, Colorado, on Route 34 kills 139
Seattle Seahawks take the field for the first time in a preseason game against the San Francisco 49ers at the newly constructed Kingdome in Seattle
Trinidad & Tobago adopts constitution
XXI Summer Olympic Games close at the Olympic Stadium in Montreal, Canada

Gunman occupying Iraqi Embassy in Paris surrenders and releases eight hostages. As the gunman is led away, Iraqi guards open fire, wounding him and killing a policeman. Police return fire, killing one Iraqi and wounding three others.
Maria de Lourdes Pintasilgo forms a government in Portugal
Buttevant Rail Disaster kills 18 and injures dozens of train passengers in County Cork, Ireland
East German runner Waldemar Cierpinski wins his second consecutive Olympic Games marathon in 12:11:03 in Moscow
Gerd Wessig of East Germany becomes the first male to set a high jump world record of 2.36 m (7 ft 9 in) at the Moscow Olympics
Octavian(later known asAugustus) enters Alexandria, Egypt, bringing it under the control of the Roman Republic
Justinian Ibecomes the sole ruler of the Byzantine Empire after the death of his uncle Justin I
Peace Treaty of Venice: EmperorFrederick Iand Pope Alexander III
Robert the Bruce's siege of Carlisle Castle ends in failure in Cumbria, England, during the First War of Scottish Independence
Henry Tudor's army sails to England (futureHenry VII)
Christopher Columbuslands for the first time in South America near the mouth of the Orinoco River in modern-day Venezuela
Assassination of FrenchKing Henry IIIby friar Jacques Clément
EmperorFerdinand IIdemands Austrian Protestants convert to Catholicism
Battle of Staffarda: French army led by Nicolas Catinat defeats the Duchy of Savoy led byVictor Amadeus II(Nine Years' War)
After being surrounded, TsarPeter the Greatflees Azov
Monarch Georg Ludwig becomes KingGeorge Iof England
French naval scientific expedition ordered byLouis XVIand led by La Pérouse with 220 men departs Brest (lost in the Pacific in 1788)
Battle of the Nile: British Royal Navy under AdmiralHoratio Nelsonattacks and decimates the French fleet at Aboukir Bay off the Nile Delta, Egypt
Charles Darwinreturns to Bahia in Brazil as part of his homeward journey on the HMS Beagle
Harriet Quimbypasses her pilot's test and becomes the first US woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate
Omar Bradley, aged 18, begins his education at West Point Military Academy
EmperorWilhelm IIof Germany declares war on his cousin TsarNicholas IIof Russia in WWI
Marcus Garveyand others found the Universal Negro Improvement Association in Jamaica with the aim of creating a Black-governed nation in Africa
Queen Wilhelminaopens the first Air Fair in Amsterdam
Influential country music group theCarter Familymakes its first recording with producer Ralph Peer in Bristol, Tennessee
Babe Ruthhits home run #42 in the Yankees' 12-1 win vs. the St. Louis Browns, putting him 4 weeks ahead of his 1927 pace
Dutch colonial regime in Batavia arrestsSukarno
National Recovery Administration (NRA) is founded withHugh S. Johnsonas its first director
New York's future Hall of Fame pitcherCarl Hubbellsets an MLB record for consecutive scoreless innings at 45 1/3 as the Giants lose 3-1 to the Boston Braves
XI Summer Olympic Games are opened byAdolf Hitlerin Berlin
NY Yankees pitcherLefty Gomezsets MLB record for most walks in a shutout, issuing 11 in a 9-0 win vs. St. Louis Browns
Adam Clayton Powell Jr.is elected as the first African American congressman from New York
Anne Frank's last diary entry; she is arrested 3 days later
NY Giants outfielderMel Otthits historic 500th MLB home run off Braves Johnny Hutchings, joins Babe Ruth and Jimmy Foxx as only players to reach milestone
US PresidentHarry Trumanestablishes Atomic Energy Commission (AEC)
David Ben-Gurion's Mapai Party wins the Israeli parliamentary election
Fidel Castrois arrested in Cuba
Tunku Abdul Rahmanforms Malacca government
Gil Hodgeshits his 13th MLB career grand slam, a National League record and the last by a Brooklyn Dodgers player
PopeJohn XXIIIpublishes the encyclical "Sacerdotii Nostri Primordia"
Aretha Franklin's first recording session for Columbia Records in New York City
Failed assassination of PresidentKwame Nkrumahof Ghana
Scottish Lotus driverJim Clarkwins the German Grand Prix at the Nürburgring to clinch his second F1 World Championship
Yakubu Gowonbecomes Nigerian Head of State after a military coup d'état

Three-day Atlantic City Pop Festival opens at the Atlantic City Race Course in Hamilton Township, New Jersey; performers include Procol Harum,Joni Mitchell, Chicago, Santana,Little Richard, Booker T and the MG's, Sir Douglas Quintet,Janis Joplin, Jefferson Airplane,B.B. King, The Byrds,Creedence Clearwater Revival, Tim Buckley, Hugh Masekela,Dr. John, and The Mothers of Invention; attendance is estimated at 110,000
Future Hall of Fame outfielderWillie Stargellhits three doubles and two homers in a 20-10 Pirates win against the Braves at Atlanta Stadium
"TheSonny & CherComedy Hour" debuts on CBS-TV, with comic actor and singerJimmy Duranteas guest

George Harrison's benefit concerts for Bangladesh (two shows) take place at Madison Square Garden, NYC; performers includeBob Dylan,Ringo Starr,Ravi Shankar,Eric Clapton,Billy Preston, and Leon Russell
Richard Pettywins the Dixie 500 in Atlanta, Georgia, to become the first NASCAR driver to win $1 million in career earnings
First article exposing the Watergate scandal by Bernstein and Woodward is published in The Washington Post
Struggling ABA club Virginia Squires trades star forwardJulius Ervingto New York Nets for George Carter and cash
Reigning world F1 championNiki Laudaof Austria suffers a near-fatal crash during the German Grand Prix at the Nürburgring motorsports complex, Nürburg, Germany
Former CIA Lockheed U-2 pilotFrancis Gary Powerscrashes the news helicopter he is flying in Los Angeles, killing himself and a cameraman
San Francisco Giants' first basemanWillie McCoveyhits a National League record 18th MLB career grand slam
Atlanta Braves trounce Cincinnati Reds 16-4 and haltPete Rose's National League record hitting streak at 44 games
"Endless Love" single released byDiana RossandLionel Richie(Billboard Song of the Year 1981, Billboard Greatest Song Duet of All-Time)
Results of the Domesday inquiry are presented toWilliam the Conquerorin Salisbury (the date of compilation and the Great Domesday are historically contestable)
English chemistJoseph Priestleydiscovers oxygen by isolating it in its gaseous state
British army under General Cornwallis occupies Yorktown, Virginia
Slavery Abolition Act 1833 comes into effect, abolishing slavery across the British Empire
Publius Helvius Pertinax, Roman Emperor (Year of the Five Emperors), born in Alba Pompeia, Italia (d. 193)
Kōgon, 1st Japanese Emperor of the Northern Court (1331-3) during the period of Northern and Southern Courts, born in Japan (d. 1364)
Go-Komatsu, 100th Emperor of Japan (1392-1412) and the 6th and final Emperor of the Northern Court, born in Japan (d. 1433)
Jan van Scorel, Dutch painter and architect, born in Schoorl, Netherlands (d. 1562)

Ahmed II, 21st Sultan of Turkey (1691-95), born in Istanbul, Ottoman Empire (d. 1695) [or born Feb 25, 1643]

Robert Todd Lincoln, American politician, lawyer, Captain (Union Army), diplomat, businessman, and son of President Abraham Lincoln, born in Springfield, Illinois (d. 1926)

William Orlamond, Danish actor (Flesh and the Devil, The Wind), born in Copenhagen, Denmark (d. 1957)

Ilya Ivanovich Ivanov, Russian biologist who perfected the artificial insemination of animals and showed that his technology allowed one stallion to fertilize up to 500 mares, born in Shchigry, Kursk Oblast, Russia (d. 1932)
